name: Kinshasa
notes: Capital city with province-level status. Formerly Léopoldville, renamed Kinshasa in 1966. During the Zaire era (1971–1997) retained the name Kinshasa. The 1963 constitution briefly created 21 provinces but these were consolidated to 8+Kinshasa in 1966.

name: Bandundu
notes: Created in the 1966 consolidation from parts of the former Léopoldville province. Retained the same name during the Zaire era. Split into Kwango, Kwilu, and Mai-Ndombe in 2015.

name: Bas-Congo
notes: Created in the 1966 consolidation. Renamed Bas-Zaïre during the Zaire era (1971–1997), then reverted to Bas-Congo (1997–2015), then renamed Kongo Central in 2015.

name: Équateur
notes: Created in the 1966 consolidation from the former Coquilhatville/Équateur province. Retained the same name through all eras. Split into Équateur, Mongala, Nord-Ubangi, Sud-Ubangi, and Tshuapa in 2015.

name: Kasaï-Occidental
notes: Created from the western part of the former Lusambo/Kasai province. Retained the same name through all eras. Split into Kasaï and Kasaï-Central in 2015.

name: Kasaï-Oriental
notes: Created from the eastern part of the former Lusambo/Kasai province. Retained the same name through all eras. Split into Kasaï-Oriental, Lomami, and Sankuru in 2015.

name: Katanga
notes: Continued from the former Élisabethville/Katanga province. Renamed Shaba during the Zaire era (1971–1997), then reverted to Katanga. Split into Haut-Katanga, Haut-Lomami, Lualaba, and Tanganyika in 2015.

name: Kivu
notes: Continued from the former Costermansville/Kivu province. In 1988 split into three separate provinces: Maniema, Nord-Kivu, and Sud-Kivu. This province no longer existed after 1988.

name: Orientale
notes: Continued from the former Stanleyville/Orientale province. Renamed Haut-Zaïre during the Zaire era (1971–1997), then reverted to Orientale. Split into Bas-Uélé, Haut-Uélé, Ituri, and Tshopo in 2015.
